page: Amazon Web Services c6g.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton2 processor, utilizing the Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance provides 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. The processor features 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support. It does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12,132.72 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 5,457.0. The instance demonstrates capable memory bandwidth, reaching 472.95 GB/sec for cached read-only operations and 124.9 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 960.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 5,241.48. Cryptographic and compression tasks show lower relative throughput, with weak performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, though database operations and Redis SET operations remain stable, with Redis reaching 9.39 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture offers a high density of dedicated physical cores relative to its resource footprint, making it a viable option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require x86 instruction sets. The instance is well-suited for application servers, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and containerized microservices. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases, heavy single-threaded processing, or large language model inference, where its text generation speeds remain low.
description: Amazon Web Services c6g.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton2 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, with no hyperthreading. The hardware includes 32 MB of L3 cache and supports AES cryptography, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Network performance is rated at a baseline of 2 Gbps. Benchmarks show average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12,132.72, and strong cached memory read bandwidth of 472.95 GB/sec. However, single-core metrics and compression speeds are weak. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads, making it suitable for static web serving, software compilation, and containerized applications, while being less optimal for memory-heavy or single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, suitable for multi-threaded applications.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized virtual server featuring eight dedicated physical cores and balanced multi-threaded performance for containerized workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton2 ARM64 processor running at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory and 32 MB of L3 cache
Delivers 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core performance and cached memory read speeds
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and microservices
Lacks local storage, dedicated GPUs, and x86 vector extensions
